texto: After all, when you come across the word prothalamium and find it means a preliminary nuptial song and a yaffle is a green woodpecker-well, I ask you! You cannot let matters rest there-or can you? Peradventure you will develop into lexicomaniacs.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de E. Norman Torry que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “After all, when you come across the word prothalamium and find it means a preliminary nuptial son…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, books, lexicon, com foco em lexicon e words.

texto: A library should fill our leisure with adventure. It is a refuge from the commonplace and the dull, a sanctuary where all the trials, the tribulations, and the boredoms of the outer world are forbidden and where such an evil thing as a tax-collector may be forgotten and, peradventure, forgiven.
explicacao: Orientação direta de E. Norman Torry sobre temas ligados a reflexao. A afirmação “A library should fill our leisure with adventure. It is a refuge from the commonplace and the dul…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, bibliophile, libraries, útil em momentos de libraries e library.

texto: A fig for your precious society with its bridge parties, its inane chatter, its cheap mentality; its dances and vulgar banquets; its snobbery and cheap pretension. The humblest library can show you upon a single shelf better society and far more select company than all the drawing-rooms of Europe, America, and South Africa.
explicacao: Citação de E. Norman Torry sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “A fig for your precious society with its bridge parties, its inane chatter, its cheap mentality; …” articula saude-mental, bibliomania, libraries em registro de saude mental, com relevância para contextos como libraries e library.
